Classify indoor/outdoor states from mobile phone signaling data using machine learning. Extract 9 features: small cell percentage, inter-location handover rate, inter-cell handover rate, building coverage ratio, building height, duration, speed, trajectory complexity (location-based), and trajectory complexity (cell-based). Train with 5-fold stratified cross-validation. Expected output: bar chart (8x6 inches, 300 dpi) showing precision, recall, and F1 score with values labeled on top of bars (colors: #5d8ca8, #d5695d, #ffc15e), saved as workspace/indoor_classification_metrics.png. Target metrics: precision > 0.85, recall > 0.85, F1 > 0.85.

Extract visited location segments from mobile phone trajectory data using 150-meter spatial clustering threshold. Implement iterative clustering that groups consecutive GPS points within 150m distance, compute representative location as mode of coordinates. Expected output: histogram (10x6 inches, 300 dpi) showing distribution of location segments per user with color #5d8ca8 and black edges, saved as workspace/location_segmentation.png. Print total segments, number of users, and average segments per user.

Analyze temporal characteristics of mobile phone signaling data by computing time intervals between consecutive records for each user. Calculate cumulative distribution function and identify quartiles (Q1, median, Q3). Expected output: log-scale CDF plot (10x6 inches, 300 dpi) with time intervals on x-axis and cumulative percentage on y-axis, showing curve in color #d5695d (linewidth=2.5) with three vertical dashed lines marking Q1 (green), median (blue), and Q3 (orange) with legend showing values in seconds, saved as workspace/time_interval_distribution.png. Print Q1, median, and Q3 values.

Calculate time-weighted accuracy metrics for indoor state predictions using interpolated duration estimation. Apply 0.43/0.57 weight ratio to previous/next time intervals to estimate duration for each record. Compute time-weighted precision (sum of durations for true positives / sum of durations for all positive predictions), recall (sum of durations for true positives / sum of durations for all actual positives), and F1 score. Expected output: bar chart (8x6 inches, 300 dpi) showing three metrics with values labeled on top (colors: #5d8ca8, #d5695d, #ffc15e), saved as workspace/time_weighted_accuracy.png. Print time-weighted precision, recall, and F1 score values.

Analyze temporal variation of indoor state classification accuracy across 24-hour period. Divide day into 2-hour bins (0-2, 2-4, ..., 22-24), calculate time-weighted F1 score for each period using 0.43/0.57 interpolation weights. Expected output: line plot (12x6 inches, 300 dpi) showing F1 score (y-axis, range 0-1) vs time period (x-axis) with markers (color=#d5695d, linewidth=2, markersize=8), x-axis labels rotated 45 degrees, saved as workspace/temporal_f1_variation.png. Print mean F1 score and standard deviation across all time periods.

Detect activity locations from geolocated social media data using spatial clustering with 100-meter distance threshold and minimum 3 points per cluster. Identify home and work locations by selecting clusters with highest activity probabilities. Expected output: print total number of detected clusters and activity probability statistics.

Compare three probability combination methods (sum-based, OR-based, product-based) for integrating land use and time-use probabilities to predict activity locations. Calculate combined probabilities for home and work activities using each method. Expected output: visualization showing probability distributions for all three methods with comparative statistics, saved as workspace/fuzzy_comparison.png (12x8 inches, 150 dpi).

Identify cross-border commuters in the Greater Luxembourg Region by detecting users whose home location is outside Luxembourg but work location is inside Luxembourg. Aggregate results by origin country. Expected output: bar chart showing commuter counts by origin country (horizontal bars), saved as workspace/cross_border_commuters.png (10x6 inches, 150 dpi). Print total commuters and percentage breakdown by country.

Implement hierarchical activity location detection with three levels: first identify the most probable country based on aggregated probabilities, then determine the specific municipality within that country, finally pinpoint the exact cluster location. Expected output: print detection results showing country, municipality, and cluster coordinates for each user with confidence scores.

Validate activity location predictions against expert-labeled ground truth by calculating macro-averaged F1 score, precision, and recall at country level. Expected output: classification report showing per-country metrics and overall macro-averaged scores. Target metrics: macro F1 > 0.75, precision > 0.70, recall > 0.70.

Implement multi-scale spatial clustering with hierarchical activity location detection. Test multiple spatial scales (50m, 100m, 200m, 500m), select optimal scale per user using silhouette score, then apply three-level hierarchy (country → municipality → cluster) with sum-based probability logic to identify cross-border commuters. Expected output: 2x2 grid visualization (16x12 inches, 150 dpi) showing (1) optimal scale distribution bar chart, (2) activity probability scatter plot (home vs work), (3) cross-border commuters by origin horizontal bar chart, (4) home location spatial distribution map, saved as workspace/task1_multiscale_detection.png. Print total users, cross-border commuters count and percentage, and origin country distribution.

Build a spatially-weighted regression model to predict cross-border commuting flows. Calculate spatial features (distance to Luxembourg border, population density, distance to rail), implement regression with adaptive spatial bandwidth, evaluate with R² and MAE, and perform spatial autocorrelation analysis on residuals. Expected output: 2x2 grid visualization (16x12 inches, 150 dpi) showing (1) actual commuter distribution map, (2) predicted commuter distribution map, (3) residuals distribution map, (4) actual vs predicted scatter plot with R² in title, saved as workspace/task2_gwr_analysis.png. Target metrics: R² > 0.60, MAE < 50.

Apply probabilistic inference to optimize activity location detection. Define time-dependent priors from time-use surveys, compute posterior probabilities using conjugate priors, select locations considering both probability and uncertainty. Expected output: 2x2 grid visualization (14x10 inches, 150 dpi) showing (1) posterior probability distribution histogram for home/work, (2) probability vs uncertainty scatter plot, (3) average home probability by time period bar chart, (4) location selection score histogram, saved as workspace/task3_bayesian_inference.png. Print mean posterior probability and uncertainty.

Develop ensemble learning models for activity location prediction. Integrate multiple classifiers, perform 5-fold cross-validation with macro F1 scoring, analyze feature importance. Expected output: bar chart (10x6 inches, 150 dpi) showing feature importance scores, saved as workspace/task4_ensemble.png. Print cross-validation F1 scores with mean and standard deviation. Target: macro F1 > 0.70.

Construct and analyze a spatiotemporal commuting network. Build directed graph from home-work flows, compute degree and betweenness centrality, apply community detection. Expected output: 1x2 grid visualization (16x6 inches, 150 dpi) showing (1) commuting network graph with nodes and directed edges, (2) degree centrality vs betweenness centrality scatter plot, saved as workspace/task5_network.png. Print number of nodes, edges, and top 5 central nodes.

Identify clusters of restaurants (餐饮服务) in Beijing that are mutually accessible within 10-minute drive time using the road network. Calculate cluster statistics including average inter-POI travel time and spatial compactness. Expected output: scatter plot showing top 5 clusters in different colors (red, blue, green, orange, purple) with legend indicating cluster sizes, saved as workspace/mining_task1_clusters.png (12x10 inches, 150 dpi). CSV file workspace/mining_task1_results.csv containing cluster_id, size, avg_travel_time_min, and spatial_extent_m for each cluster.

Discover regional co-location patterns in Chongqing where shopping (购物服务), dining (餐饮服务), and entertainment (体育休闲服务) POIs co-occur within 15-minute travel time neighborhoods. Rank patterns by participation index (PI) and location relevance (LR) scores. Expected output: map visualization (12x10 inches, 150 dpi) showing all POI types as scatter points (shopping=blue, dining=red, entertainment=green) with top 3 co-location region centers marked as black stars with yellow edges and labeled R1, R2, R3, saved as workspace/mining_task2_colocation.png. CSV file workspace/mining_task2_results.csv containing center_node, center_x, center_y, shopping_count, dining_count, entertainment_count, PI, LR, and score for top 3 regions.

Generate 5, 10, 15-minute travel time accessibility zones from all hospitals (医疗保健服务 > 综合医院) in Kunming. Calculate coverage area for each time threshold and identify underserved areas. Expected output: map visualization (14x12 inches, 150 dpi) showing three overlapping accessibility zones with color gradient (5-min=#ff9999, 10-min=#ffcc99, 15-min=#ffff99, alpha=0.3), hospital locations marked with red '+' symbols (size=150, linewidth=3), saved as workspace/mining_task3_isochrones.png. CSV file workspace/mining_task3_results.csv containing threshold_min, reachable_nodes, and coverage_area_km2 for each threshold.

Compare POI spatial distribution patterns between Beijing and Salt Lake City by analyzing the relationship between road network centrality and POI density. Calculate network node centrality metrics, count commercial POIs (购物服务, 餐饮服务, 公司企业) within 500m of each node, and perform correlation analysis. Expected output: side-by-side scatter plots (14x6 inches, 150 dpi) showing centrality vs POI count for both cities with correlation coefficients displayed in titles, saved as workspace/mining_task4_comparison.png. CSV file workspace/mining_task4_results.csv containing city, spearman_rho, and p_value for each city.

Find optimal locations for 3 new emergency service facilities in Chongqing to minimize maximum travel time to any existing residential area (商务住宅 > 住宅区), subject to minimum 20-minute separation between facilities. Expected output: map visualization (12x10 inches, 150 dpi) showing residential areas as light blue scatter points (size=30, alpha=0.5) and 3 optimal facility locations as red stars (size=300) with black edges (linewidth=2) labeled as Facility 1, 2, 3, saved as workspace/mining_task5_facilities.png. CSV file workspace/mining_task5_results.csv containing facility_id, node_id, and max_travel_time_min for each facility.

Perform spatial interpolation for simulated disease risk data across 4 spatial strata. Calculate population-weighted variograms for each stratum, fit spherical models, and predict risk values at unsampled locations using stratified prediction with population correction. Expected output: three-panel visualization (15x4 inches, 300 dpi) showing (1) true risk heatmap with stratum boundaries, (2) predicted risk heatmap for test locations with stratum boundaries, (3) prediction error histogram with vertical line at zero, saved as workspace/TASK_28052177_001_result.png. Performance metrics: MAE < 0.0025, RMSE < 0.0035, Pearson r > 0.85.

Compare multiple spatial interpolation approaches for disease risk prediction. Evaluate stratified vs non-stratified methods and population-weighted vs standard variogram estimation. Calculate ME, MAE, RMSE, and Pearson correlation for each method on the test set. Expected output: 2x2 grid of boxplots (12x10 inches, 300 dpi) showing prediction error distributions by stratum (I, II, III, IV) for each method with RMSE values in titles, saved as workspace/TASK_28052177_002_result.png. Best method should achieve RMSE < 0.0035, second-best RMSE < 0.0038.

Quantify the effectiveness of spatial stratification for disease risk analysis. Calculate stratification power metric (q-statistic measuring variance reduction) and assess within-stratum spatial autocorrelation. Expected output: 2x3 grid visualization (15x10 inches, 300 dpi) showing (1) true risk heatmap with stratum boundaries (green lines), (2) observed rate heatmap with stratum boundaries, (3) strata map with color coding, (4) bar chart of mean rate by stratum with error bars, (5) bar chart comparing spatial autocorrelation values across strata and globally (red for global, blue for strata), (6) text panel displaying q-statistic and global autocorrelation value, saved as workspace/TASK_28052177_003_result.png. Target metrics: q-statistic > 0.65, global spatial autocorrelation > 0.45.
